schema_notes: Each expectation has: text, kind ('outcome'|'process'), applicability ('both'|'with_skill_only'|'without_skill_only'), and optional oracle ref.

Outcome expectations are graded for both configs and contribute to the headline pass-rate delta. Process expectations record HOW the work was done and are reported separately; they do not pollute the comparison.
An 'oracle' references a slot expected field. Mechanically verifiable assertions are graded by scripts/finalize_grading.py from transcript + ground truth, not by the LLM grader.

cases.title: Disambiguate a common member name
cases.prompt: Find all usages of the {{DISAMBIGUATE_MEMBER.symbol}} property on {{DISAMBIGUATE_MEMBER.containingType}}, not every {{DISAMBIGUATE_MEMBER.symbol}} in the repo. List each call site with file path and line number.
cases.expected_output: A usage list scoped to the exact member identity.

expectations.text: Reports usages in every expected file (recall vs DISAMBIGUATE_MEMBER.expected.expectedFiles)
Does not report any decoy file as a usage (precision vs DISAMBIGUATE_MEMBER.expected.decoyFiles)
Reports at least DISAMBIGUATE_MEMBER.expected.minimumUsageSites distinct file:line citations
Every reported file:line citation resolves to a real line on disk
Resolves the member with containingType or fileHint before scanning usages

source.summary: Member identity precision under grep-noisy names

cases.title: Disambiguate callers of a common function name
cases.prompt: Find callers of {{OVERLOADED_OR_COMMON_FUNCTION.containingType}}.{{OVERLOADED_OR_COMMON_FUNCTION.symbol}}(), not every function named {{OVERLOADED_OR_COMMON_FUNCTION.symbol}} in the project. Show the call hierarchy.
cases.expected_output: A call hierarchy rooted at the exact function identity.

expectations.text: Lists every expected caller fqName
Reports at least OVERLOADED_OR_COMMON_FUNCTION.expected.minimumCallers callers
Does not report callers of unrelated functions named the same as the target symbol (precision: every reported caller actually invokes the target containingType)
Disambiguates the function using containingType, kind, or fileHint

source.summary: Function caller precision under overloaded names

cases.title: Prove exhaustive cross-workspace references
cases.prompt: Find every reference to {{CROSS_MODULE_CLASS.symbol}} across the entire workspace. Tell me whether the search was exhaustive — did it cover every candidate file, or was it sampled/truncated?
cases.expected_output: A complete reference report with explicit search completeness metadata.

expectations.text: Reports at least CROSS_MODULE_CLASS.expected.minimumReferences references
Names every expected consumer file at least once
Names every expected consumer module
States the search completeness (exhaustive vs truncated/sampled) explicitly, not implicitly
Backs the completeness claim with structural metadata from the tool, not narrative assertion

cases.title: Trace every sealed hierarchy implementation
cases.prompt: List every implementation of the sealed interface {{SEALED_HIERARCHY.symbol}}. For each implementation, show its file location and which module it belongs to.
cases.expected_output: A complete subtype report with module attribution.

expectations.text: Lists every expected implementation by fqName (recall)
Does not report any subtype that is not in the expected set (precision)
Correctly attributes each implementation to its host module
Uses semantic resolution (not regex over `class.*Konstrained`) to discover subtypes

cases.title: Safely rename a symbol across files
cases.prompt: Rename {{RENAME_TARGET.symbol}} to {{RENAME_TARGET.newName}} across the entire workspace. Show me the edit plan before applying. After applying, confirm no compile errors were introduced.
cases.expected_output: A multi-file rename plan and validated post-edit status.

expectations.text: Edit plan lists every file in RENAME_TARGET.expected.affectedFiles (recall)
Edit plan does not list extra files outside the expected set (precision)
Post-rename compile status is reported, captured from the build (oracle: RENAME_TARGET.expected.compileCommand)
Import statements are updated, not just the declaration site
Uses kast_rename (not find-and-replace, sed, or manual edits)

cases.title: Edit and validate atomically
cases.prompt: Add a @Deprecated annotation with message 'Use {{RENAME_TARGET.newName}} instead' to the {{RENAME_TARGET.symbol}} interface declaration. Confirm the file still compiles after the edit.
cases.expected_output: A validated edit report with diagnostics evidence.

expectations.text: After the run, the target file actually contains @Deprecated with the expected message string
Post-edit compile status is reported, captured from the build
No unrelated files were modified by this run
Uses kast_write_and_validate (not raw edit/create tool)

cases.title: Summarize a large class structurally
cases.prompt: Summarize the public API of {{LARGE_CLASS.symbol}} — list every public type, interface, enum, and data class it contains, with their member signatures.
cases.expected_output: A scaffold-rooted API summary for a large class.

expectations.text: Lists every member in LARGE_CLASS.expected.publicMembers (recall)
Lists every type in LARGE_CLASS.expected.nestedTypes (recall)
Does not list any member or nested type not in the expected sets (precision)
Transcript output is at least 50% smaller than the raw file content (oracle: LARGE_CLASS.expected.rawFileLineCount)
Uses kast_scaffold (not raw file read) as the primary information source

cases.title: Discover modules in one semantic call
cases.prompt: List every module in this workspace and how many Kotlin source files each contains.
cases.expected_output: A module inventory with Kotlin source file counts.

expectations.text: Names every module in MODULE_LIST.modules
Reported file count for each module matches MODULE_LIST.expected.moduleFileCounts within tolerance
Does not report modules outside MODULE_LIST.modules
Completes in a single semantic tool call (kast_workspace_files), not iterative directory traversal

cases.title: Trace two-level deletion impact
cases.prompt: I want to delete {{OVERLOADED_OR_COMMON_FUNCTION.containingType}}.{{OVERLOADED_OR_COMMON_FUNCTION.symbol}}(). Show me every direct caller, then for each caller show its callers (depth 2). Identify which of those callers are in test files vs production code.
cases.expected_output: A bounded depth-2 caller impact report with production/test classification.

expectations.text: Output shows a 2-level call hierarchy (direct callers + callers-of-callers)
Every reported caller is classified as test or production based on its file path
Direct callers list includes every expectedCallerFqName
If the depth-2 result was bounded (e.g., max-depth or max-callers cap reached), the bound is reported in the output
Resolves the exact function (containingType disambiguation) before tracing callers

cases.title: Trace cross-module symbol flow
cases.prompt: Trace how {{CROSS_MODULE_CLASS.symbol}} flows from its definition in {{CROSS_MODULE_CLASS.file}} to its consumers in other modules. Show the cross-module dependency chain.
cases.expected_output: A concrete file-to-file flow report across module boundaries.

expectations.text: Names at least one expected consumer file from CROSS_MODULE_CLASS.expected.expectedConsumerFiles
Output contains concrete file:line citations, not just module names
Output makes the direction of the flow explicit (definition → consumer), not just an unordered set
Uses scaffold + references + callers in sequence (rather than grep) to assemble the flow
